channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: JuJu Watkins and No. 4 USC visit No. 2 UCLA in winner-take-all game for Big Ten regular-season title
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It’s probably not what the Big Ten expected when the league opened its doors to UCLA and USC this season: two West Coast schools dominating the women’s basketball standings.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Knapp goes from a 59 to a 70 but still has the lead in the Cognizant Classic
item.description: Jake Knapp still has the lead at the Cognizant Classic even though it felt like a tough day at the office.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Times Square to host returns of Ryan Garcia and Devin Haney in separate bouts in May 2 tripleheader
item.description: Times Square will stage a boxing tripleheader featuring the returns of Ryan Garcia and Devin Haney, along with a title defense for Teofimo Lopez, on May 2.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mahoney, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: College star Luke Clanton easily makes the cut in Cognizant Classic and earns PGA Tour card
item.description: PALM BEACH GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Florida State junior Luke Clanton only needed to make the cut Friday in the Cognizant Classic to earn a PGA Tour card. That was never in doubt.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indy 500 champ Newgarden becomes 1st driver to sign with Klutch Sports Group management agency
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Two-time reigning Indianapolis 500 winner champion Josef Newgarden has signed with Klutch Sports Group, the Rich Paul-founded management agency that also represents LeBron James, Darius Garland, Anthony Davis, Draymond Green and USC star JuJu Watkins.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LSU track athlete dead at 18 after fiery car wreck on school's campus
item.description: Dillon Reidenauer, an LSU pole vaulter, was one of two people killed in a fiery car crash on the school's campus this week. She was 18 years old.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jose Mourinho banned for four matches and fined after Turkish referees comment
item.description: ISTANBUL, Turkey (AP) — Fenerbahce manager Jose Mourinho was given a four-match ban and fined by the Turkish Football Federation following his comments about Turkish referees after a match at Galatasaray.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In dominant triple-double performance, Pelicans' Zion Williamson provides reminder of his talent
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The injuries have taken on toll on Zion Williamson during his six-year NBA career. So have the losses. But on nights like Thursday, there are tantalizing reminders of how much talent the 2019 No. 1 overall pick still possesses.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Women athletes plead for Senate Democrats to help pass Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act
item.description: Multiple women athletes have sent their pleas to Democratic senators to vote in favor of the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boys Basketball: Carlsbad 73, Cathedral 61
item.description:
Carlsbad basketball had a repeat on its mind: a goal to get back to the Open Division title game.
Consider it mission accomplished after a 73 to 61 win over a hard charging Cathedral team.
Jett Kenady was the difference for the Lancers on Tuesday night, scoring 24 points. Jake Hall added 22.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, Fox 5

item.title: Maine lawmaker censured for posting picture of trans student on Facebook
item.description: (NewsNation) — A state representative in Maine was censured by other members of the legislature after posting a picture of a trans teen girl on her public Facebook page. The resolution passed 75-70 in a party-line vote, the Portland Press-Herald reported. Per Maine House rules, Republican Rep.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Utah State sweeps San Diego State, 79-71
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - For just the second time first since joining the Mountain West Conference, Utah State sweeps San Diego State. Dexter Akanno scored a career-high 26 points, making 7-of-9 three-pointers to lead the Aggies to a 79-71 win over the Aztecs Saturday night at The Spectrum.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kent Johnson scores 2 goals, the Blue Jackets beat the Blackhawks 5-1 to end a 4-game losing streak
item.description: Zach Werenski scored and had two assists and Kent Johnson scored twice for the Columbus Blue Jackets in a 5-1 victory against the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday to end a four-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kylie Kravig Makes History as CMU’s All-Time Assists Leader
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — When the Colorado Mesa Mavericks need a play, there’s one player they trust to have the ball in her hands—Kylie Kravig. Kravig has been the floor general for the Mavericks all season, averaging six assists per game.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Red Rocks clinch Big 12 regular season title
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The University of Utah claimed the Big 12 Conference’s first gymnastics regular-season title. The Red Rocks earned the title Friday after a 198.075-197.525 victory against Denver to go 6-0 during league play.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Thunder cruise past Jazz in first game after break
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 21 points and the West-leading Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Utah Jazz 130-107 on Friday night for their eighth victory in nine games. Chet Holmgren added 20 points for the Thunder.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
